sources.notes: Statement and constraints: construct b or report impossible; b_i can be large.
Luogu mirror used to cross-check statement and constraints.
Used to confirm the validity conditions and greedy construction.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Given target values a_i, decide whether they can be the (n-i+1)-mex of each prefix of a nonnegative array b, and construct one valid b.
annotation.constraints: t <= 1e4, sum n <= 2e5, a_i <= 1e9, and constructed b_i must fit the statement bound.
annotation.core_idea: After i elements are chosen, the requested k-mex has k = n - i + 1. This forces a_i to be nonincreasing, and at zero-based position i it must satisfy n - i - 1 <= a_i <= n. If a drops, output a large dummy value so the missing set below the new answer is preserved while k decreases. If a stays equal, output an unused value below a_i that is not itself one of the target a-values, filling exactly one currently missing small number.
tricks: derive tight bounds on kth missing value
use a large dummy value on decreases
avoid placing values that must remain missing later

solution_variants.name: monotone-mex-greedy-construction
solution_variants.summary: Check monotonicity and range constraints, then scan left to right: emit INF on strict drops and otherwise greedily emit an available non-target value below the current target.

tags.evidence: The solution is an explicit construction of b from necessary monotonicity and range conditions.
When a_i stays equal, the construction greedily consumes an available missing number below a_i while preserving future target values as missing.
The invariant is entirely phrased through kth missing nonnegative values of prefixes.
